perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement do not extend to information that is: (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure or subsequently becomes public ly known through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) discovered or created by the Receiving Party before disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) learned by the Receiving Party through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclosing Party's representatives; or (d) is disclosed by Receiving Party with Disclosing Party's prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party's obligations under this Agreement do not extend to information that is: (a) publicly known at the time of disclosure or subsequently becomes public ly known through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) discovered or created by the Receiving Party before disclosure by Disclosing Party; (c) learned by the Receiving Party through legitimate means other than from the Disclosing Party or Disclosing Party's representatives; (d) is disclosed by Receiving Party with Disclosing Party's prior written approval; or (e) information independently developed by the Receiving Party, even if similar to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, as long as there is no direct use of the Disclosing Party's documentation.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds exception (e) to the exclusions from Confidential Information, allowing the Receiving Party to independently develop similar information. This contradicts the fundamental obligation in Section 3 to hold and maintain Confidential Information in strictest confidence, as independent development could lead to use or disclosure of information derived from confidential knowledge, even without direct use of documentation. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the level of confidentiality required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The addition of exception (e) in the changed text allows the Receiving Party to independently develop information similar to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, as long as there is no direct use of documentation. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence and shall not use it for its own benefit without prior written approval. The added exception weakens the confidentiality obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it permits the Receiving Party to benefit from similar information, even if derived from confidential knowledge, undermining the Disclosing Party's exclusive benefit.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Confidential Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party, unless required by law. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Confidential Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefit, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their b enefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Confidential Inform ation immediately if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The added phrase 'unless required by law' in Section 3 creates a potential contradiction. While seemingly reasonable, it conflicts with the absolute confidentiality obligation initially stated. Section 4 stipulates the agreement survives termination until the information is no longer a trade secret, but this 'unless required by law' clause opens a loophole, potentially requiring disclosure even while it's considered a trade secret under the original terms of the agreement in section 4. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information in the strictest confidence. The changed text adds 'unless required by law'. This creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which states that the duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall remain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret. The 'unless required by law' clause allows for disclosure even while the information is still a trade secret, directly conflicting with the original duration of the confidentiality obligation. This is an inconsistency that leads to an in-text contradiction as described in the instructions.

perturbation.original_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement and Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall r emain in effect until the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.changed_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement, however, Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall only r emain in effect for a period of 2 years after the termination of this agreement, regardless of whether the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the Time Periods clause by limiting the nondisclosure period to 2 years creates a direct in-text contradiction. The original clause states the duty continues until the information is no longer a trade secret, which could be longer than 2 years. This conflicts with the modified 2-year limit, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the confidentiality obligations. It contradicts the original intent of perpetual protection for trade secrets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The nondisclosure provisions of this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement, however, Receiving Party's duty to hold Confidential Information in confidence shall only r emain in effect for a period of 2 years after the termination of this agreement, regardless of whether the Confidential Information no longer qualifies as a trade secret or until Disclosing Party sends Receiving Party written notice releasing Receiving Party from this Agreement, whichever occurs first.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the nondisclosure period to 2 years, regardless of whether the confidential information remains a trade secret. The contradicted text is the changed text itself. The original text states the duty continues until the information is no longer a trade secret, so the changed text creates a contradiction by limiting it to 2 years, creating an in-text contradiction according to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may not be amended except in a writing signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may be amended by either party with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original agreement's amendment clause. Originally, all amendments require mutual written consent. The modified version allows unilateral amendment with just a 30-day notice. This creates a direct in-text contradiction about how the contract can be modified, undermining the principle of mutual agreement. Parties would dispute which amendment process is valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the complete understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter and supersedes all prior proposals, agreements, representations and understandings. This Agreement may be amended by either party with a 30-day written notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement's amendment clause. The original clause requires mutual written consent for amendments, while the changed version allows unilateral amendment with a 30-day written notice. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding how the contract can be modified, undermining the principle of mutual agreement and creating potential disputes over which amendment process is valid. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, solidifying the in-text contradiction as it is referring to itself in a conflicting way.
